title: MCLN Case7846381-Configuration DocumentSDN191
fullText: Configuration Document Suppress 300/04 PIX for return receiving Go to tranasaction parameters S earch fill and Select the fill actv sys dir and click copy Once click copy it will be in edit mode Change the transaction name and transaction link name and click save. Select the newly created transaction and click program template. Click add for new parm Enter all the details as below and click save. Note: Don’t give any other numbers we need to give “ 521 ”-based on this only it will work in code. Come to main page in transaction parameters and the select the newly created transaction and click edit. Enable the 521 param and click save Once saved, please verify Go to RF menu maintenance S earch as “fill” on left side-> right side go to outbound Select the newly created transaction and drag and drop to the outbound and move to top of the list and click save. Once saved, Select the new transaction, and right click to assign permissions New window will be opened to assign permissions and select the modules that we want to add Select for rf inbound and click search then add RF fill active and click save. Go to cache administration and clear all the cache. Once cache cleared, please sign out and sign in. Go to RFmenu : Outbound-> Fill actv sys Dir for RET Recv When we add a new parameter to the transaction parameter, the base transaction won't work, as shown below. I tried to open fill actv sys Dir, but it won’t work. Go to SQL developer: So, to avoid, query it in the tran_master as below and select all the tran_id. Note: except newly created transaction (fill actv sys Dir for RET Recv) In tran_master_parm all the 521 menu_parm will be null, so the base transaction will not work in RF for the below transactions. So, update the from posn ’521’ - menu_parm to ‘N’ update TRAN_MASTER_parm set menu_parm='N' where tran_id in ( 40,60,1012381, 1012364,1012380) and from_posn='521'; Once updated click commit, then the above transactions will work in the RF.

chunks.text:
[Document: MCLN Case7846381-Configuration DocumentSDN191]

Configuration Document Suppress 300/04 PIX for return receiving Go to tranasaction parameters S earch fill and Select the fill actv sys dir and click copy Once click copy it will be in edit mode Change the transaction name and transaction link name and click save. Select the newly created transaction and click program template. Click add for new parm Enter all the details as below and click save. Note: Don’t give any other numbers we need to give “ 521 ”-based on this only it will work in code. Come to main page in transaction parameters and the select the newly created transaction and click edit. Enable the 521 param and click save Once saved, please verify Go to RF menu maintenance S earch as “fill” on left side-> right side go to outbound Select the newly created transaction and drag and drop to the outbound and move to top of the list and click save. Once saved, Select the new transaction, and right click to assign permissions New window will be opened to assign permissions and select the modules that we want to add Select for rf inbound and click search then add RF fill active and click save. Go to cache administration and clear all the cache. Once cache cleared, please sign out and sign in. Go to RFmenu : Outbound-> Fill actv sys Dir for RET Recv When we add a new parameter to the transaction parameter, the base transaction won't work, as shown below. I tried to open fill actv sys Dir, but it won’t work. Go to SQL developer: So, to avoid, query it in the tran_master as below and select all the tran_id. Note: except newly created transaction (fill actv sys Dir for RET Recv) In tran_master_parm all the 521 menu_parm will be null, so the base transaction will not work in RF for the below transactions. So, update the from posn ’521’ - menu_parm to ‘N’ update TRAN_MASTER_parm set menu_parm='N' where tran_id in ( 40,60,1012381, 1012364,1012380) and from_posn='521'; Once updated click commit, then the above transactions will work in the RF.
